summary: Testing of P0 Bug Fix and P1 Feature completed successfully. P0: 'Failed to fetch employees' error fixed - GET /api/hr/employees now returns 24 employees with all required fields having default values. P1: Automatic User Deactivation feature working - terminate endpoint sets access_disable_date to 30 days, admin endpoints for pending/run deactivation check working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/hr/employees returns 200 (P0 bug fix verified)
Backend: GET /api/hr/employees returns list of 24 employees
Backend: Employees have notice_period_days field with default value 30
Backend: Employees have annual_leave_balance field with default value 0
Backend: Employees have sick_leave_balance field with default value 0
Backend: Pending deactivations response has correct structure (pending, overdue, recently_deactivated, counts)
Backend: Terminate endpoint sets access_disable_date to 30 days from now
Backend: Terminated employee appears in pending deactivations list
Backend: Employee status filter works correctly
Backend: Employee department filter works correctly
Frontend: Employee Master page loads without errors
Frontend: Employee table displays all 24 employees
Frontend: Stats cards show Total Employees (24), Active (21), On Probation (0)

p0_bug_fix_employee_fetch.bug_description: EmployeeResponse Pydantic model had required fields that imported records didn't have
p0_bug_fix_employee_fetch.fix_verified: Default values added to notice_period_days=30, annual_leave_balance=0, sick_leave_balance=0
p0_bug_fix_employee_fetch.verification: All 24 employees returned with proper field types and default values

components_verified: PUT /api/hr/employees/{id}/terminate - Sets access_disable_date to 30 days from termination
GET /api/admin/pending-deactivations - Returns pending, overdue, recently_deactivated lists
Background scheduler configured to run every 6 hours (user_deactivation_loop)
p1_automatic_user_deactivation.deactivation_flow_tested: Terminate employee → Check pending shows employee → Restore to active

critical_code_review_comments: EmployeeResponse model now has proper default values for notice_period_days=30, annual_leave_balance=0, sick_leave_balance=0
work_location field changed to Optional[str] to handle missing values
employment_type has default='full_time', probation_days has default=90
process_user_deactivations() correctly finds employees with passed access_disable_date
user_deactivation_loop runs every 6 hours as background task
Terminate endpoint properly calculates 30-day grace period for access_disable_date

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Employee Master page loads and displays 24 employees)

seed_data_creation: No seed data created - used existing employee 'John Test Employee' for terminate flow test, restored to active after testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: P0 Bug Fix verified: GET /api/hr/employees returns all 24 employees without errors. Default values added to EmployeeResponse for notice_period_days, annual_leave_balance, sick_leave_balance. P1 Feature verified: User deactivation flow works - terminate sets access_disable_date to 30 days, pending-deactivations endpoint shows pending employees, run-deactivation-check processes overdue employees. Background loop runs every 6 hours.
testing_method: Backend pytest (14 tests) + Playwright browser automation + curl API verification
